title: Kingdoms / Empires
description: Broad political overlays for major biblical kingdoms and empires.

features.name: United Israel
features.summary: A broad study zone for the united monarchy.
features.description: A broad study zone for Saul, David, and Solomon-era Israel. The exact footprint is schematic and should be treated as orientation, not as a fixed border.
features.caution: This overlay is schematic and reflects a broad historical imagination, not a fixed border.

features.name: Egypt
features.summary: A recurring imperial and refuge backdrop across the biblical story.
features.description: Egypt repeatedly appears as a place of refuge, oppression, diplomacy, and symbolic memory. The footprint is schematic and intentionally coarse.
features.caution: This is a broad background layer. It helps orient the reader to the biblical world but should not be read as a precise border reconstruction.

features.name: Canaanite City-States
features.summary: A fragmented network of local powers in the land before monarchy.
features.description: A schematic pre-monarchic political backdrop for Joshua and Judges. The layer stays intentionally broad because the evidence is fragmentary and shifting.
features.caution: The evidence for local control patterns is fragmented, so this layer is a cautious teaching aid rather than a settled political map.

features.name: Philistia
features.summary: The western coastal polity that frames many Samuel and Kings narratives.
features.description: A cautious regional overlay for the Philistine coastal plain. It is useful for narrative geography, not for exact border claims.
features.caution: The coastal plain shifted over time; the layer is deliberately broad.

features.name: Northern Kingdom of Israel
features.summary: The northern kingdom centered on Samaria.
features.description: The northern kingdom centered around Samaria and the hill-country core of the divided monarchy.
features.caution: Borders are simplified and intentionally broad.

features.name: Kingdom of Judah
features.summary: The southern monarchy centered on Jerusalem.
features.description: The southern kingdom centered on Jerusalem and the Judean hill country. The exact extent shifted over time and is represented broadly here.
features.caution: The exact extent shifted over time and is represented broadly here.

features.name: Aram-Damascus
features.summary: A key northern neighbor in monarchic conflict and diplomacy.
features.description: A schematic overlay for the Aramean political sphere around Damascus. It is useful for conflict context and diplomatic framing.
features.caution: Use this as a regional cue for Assyrian-era conflicts and alliances.

features.name: Assyria
features.summary: The dominant imperial backdrop for the fall of the northern kingdom.
features.description: A broad imperial overlay for the Neo-Assyrian sphere. It is schematic and intentionally oversized for study orientation.
features.caution: The imperial extent changed by reign and decade; this is a broad study layer only.

features.name: Babylon
features.summary: The imperial setting for exile and return-from-exile narratives.
features.description: The imperial power that carried Judah into exile and ruled the Neo-Babylonian world. This is a broad imperial zone rather than an exact border.
features.caution: This is a broad imperial zone rather than an exact border.

features.name: Persia
features.summary: The imperial context for return, rebuilding, and post-exilic administration.
features.description: The empire under which the return from exile occurred. The imperial area is schematic and reflects broad historical geography.
features.caution: The imperial area is schematic and reflects broad historical geography.

features.name: Greece
features.summary: The Hellenistic world that reshaped the eastern Mediterranean after Alexander.
features.description: A broad Hellenistic political-cultural overlay for study of the intertestamental world and related texts.
features.caution: This is a broad Hellenistic frame rather than a claim about precise control lines.

features.name: Rome
features.summary: The imperial context for the Gospels and Acts.
features.description: A broad Roman imperial overlay for New Testament study. It keeps the map context careful and schematic.
features.caution: This is a broad imperial frame for the New Testament rather than a reconstruction of province-by-province borders.

features.name: Roman Judea
features.summary: The Roman provincial setting behind much of the Gospels and Acts.
features.description: The Roman provincial setting behind much of the Gospels and Acts. Borders changed and the layer remains only a study approximation.
features.caution: Roman provincial borders changed and this overlay is only a study approximation.
